These guides have a larger wall thickness than the spears to increase instrument strength; the guides also include a larger dovetail tip. Arthrex, Inc. LA1-00038-EN_C. Naples, FL; 2021. SutureTag The SutureTag looping stitch creates a running ripstop to reinforce the tendon with minimal passes through the tissue. This novel stitch is lower profile, faster to complete, and stronger than a typical Krackow repair stitch. The SutureTag splits into two #2 sutures that can be easily incorporated into the FiberTakÂź button repair technique. Product Description Item Number SutureTag w/ double 14 in #2 tails and 36.6 mm Âœ circle tapered needle (white/black) AR-7274T SutureTag w/ double 14 in #2 tails and 36.6 mm Âœ circle tapered needle (white/blue/black) AR-7274TT (a) (b) (c) (d) (e) FiberWireÂź Suture I 29 FiberWireÂź and TigerWireÂź Sutures FiberWire suture is constructed of a multistranded long chain ultra-highâmolecular weight polyethylene (UHMWPE) core with a braided jacket of polyester and UHMWPE that gives FiberWire suture superior strength, 1 soft feel, and abrasion resistance 2 that is unequaled in orthopedic surgery. Suture breakage during knot tying is virtually eliminated, especially critical during arthroscopic procedures. 3 Product Description Item Number #5 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (blue) AR-7210 #5 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (blue) w/ conventional cutting needle, 48 mm, Âœ circle AR-7211 #5 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (1 blue, 1 white, 1 white/black) w/ conventional cutting needle, 48 mm, Âœ circle AR-7212 #5 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (blue) w/ conventional cutting needle, 48 mm, Âœ circle, and tapered needle, 26.5 mm, Âœ circle AR-7213 #5 FiberWire Suture, 38 in (white) AR-7215 #2 FiberWire Suture, 38
